evidence: Design required_answer_path preserves anchors, lean startup, amendment proposal, reviewed next generation, generation-aware tools, explicit evidence lifecycle, and historical regressions.
Subagent review Harvey confirmed artifacts must not normalize full pre-goal as default and must preserve all eight blocking outcomes.
findings: Current full-chain orchestration remains a bootstrap necessity because lean mode is not implemented yet; the runtime goal explicitly implements lean mode and does not treat this chain as future default.

evidence: Goal objective is to implement lean pre-goal and generation-aware reviewed replanning control, matching the approved requested_transformation.
Design and goal both state that runtime cannot mutate approved anchors and that full pre-goal remains an exception.
findings: No artifact converts the target into documentation-only guidance or a post-hoc verifier-only change.

evidence: All eight requirements required_outcomes remain blocking in design, goal, plan, and verifier scope.
Plan work packages are mainline, producing, and count as goal progress for all blocking outcomes.
findings: No required outcome is downgraded into permission, readiness, future work, support-only work, or compatibility-only work.

evidence: S1 through S8 map one-to-one to O-lean-pregoal-default, O-full-pregoal-exception, O-generation-aware-run-control, O-runtime-amendment-proposal, O-reviewed-amendment-orchestration, O-generation-aware-guard-compiler-verifier, O-evidence-import-and-invalidation, and O-historical-regression-protection.
Plan verifier.required_outcomes lists every blocking outcome.
Each required step has a step_action_alignment describing current state, producing action, allowed authority, evidence after action, and if_not_producible handling.
Work packages require repository behavior changes to schemas, skills, compiler, guard, verifier, fixtures, and tests rather than merely checking existing files.
findings: Archimedes flagged that regression tests must be behavioral, not schema-only; WP4 explicitly requires historical semantic failure fixtures.

evidence: Plan registry_bindings select workflow 'none' and allowed assignment 'Main-only', matching the delegation workflow registry.
Current user authorized pre-goal review subagents only; target runtime parallel execution is not assumed by the compiled plan.
findings: Runtime target-work subagents require a separate approved runtime work assignment before launching /goal.

evidence: Plan allowed_write_paths cover schemas/control-json, cybernetic skill protocol files, compiler/guard/verifier scripts, tests, fixtures, and the architecture note.
Plan forbids rewriting the approved requirements.control.json in this run.
findings: Authority is broad enough to implement the control-system capability rather than only write runtime evidence, while preserving approved anchors.

evidence: Subagent Darwin identified compile-time constraints: statuses must be approved, review hashes must match, all required review checks must pass, and plan/runtime writable_evidence_paths must match.
All candidate artifacts are approved and prepared for runtime compilation with exact hash binding.
findings: Final runtime compilation must still run control_chain_guard.py and using-control-json validate_control_chain.py before emitting /goal.

evidence: Plan includes schema, fixture, routing, orchestration, guard, compiler, verifier, and runtime protocol changes required to make lean startup executable.
The bootstrapping full-chain artifacts explicitly mark current full-chain use as an implementation bridge, not the target default.
Plan WP4 requires api/v2 readiness substitution, checkpoint-only full-ceiling substitution, unresolved amendment success, stale current_generation, and old-evidence reuse regressions.
Archimedes independently confirmed these are the needed historical acceptance checks.
findings: Runtime completion must not be accepted if these regressions are documented but not executable.
